texto: June is gazing at Michael with a glow about her face, a well of feeling for her husband. Leo and Stephen are also looking at their father, Leo's expression sympathetic, nearly teary. The three of them as they look at Michael are like magnets, the ties drawing each one to him nearly visible. This is what family means, Nora thinks.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Maya Lang: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “June is gazing at Michael with a glow about her face, a well of feeling for her husband. Leo and …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, family, michael pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

texto: We all spin stories. That's what we do. We want people to see certain things about us and not others. What matters is whether you let others in to the truest story, the one that's the hardest to tell.
explicacao: Citação de Maya Lang sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “We all spin stories. That's what we do. We want people to see certain things about us and not oth…” articula reflexao, stories, others em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
